packages/core/src/config.ts.summary: hAIve project configuration — .ai/haive.config.json In autopilot mode, hAIve operates with zero human intervention: - Memories go directly to `validated` (no approval cycle) - `haive sync` auto-approves proposed memories after the delay - The MCP server saves a session recap automatically on exit - `get_briefing` auto-generates a minimal project context if none exists Multi-repo support: - crossRepoSources: pull shared memories from other repos on haive sync - contractFiles: watch API contract files for breaking changes - hubPath: local path to a shared team-knowledge hub repo

Service pour initialiser les données par défaut des produits : - Unités de mesure (par tenant - chaque tenant a ses propres unités) - Modèles d'attributs (centralisés dans le tenant SYSTEM - partagés par tous)

Architecture optimisée :

sandaga-monorepo/sandaga_frontend/src/ut.summary: Validation utilities with security considerations These functions are designed to be completely REGEX-FREE to prevent ReDoS (Regular Expression Denial of Service) attacks Uses only string operations with O(n) complexity

exports.name: isValidEmail
exports.description: Validates an email address format safely WITHOUT using regex Uses only string operations with O(n) complexity - immune to ReDoS  @param email - The email string to validate @returns true if the email is valid, false otherwise

exports.name: isValidOptionalEmail
exports.description: Validates an optional email (empty string or undefined is considered valid)  @param email - The email string to validate (can be empty/undefined) @returns true if the email is empty or valid, false otherwise

exports.name: isValidNumberString
exports.description: Validates a number string format safely WITHOUT using regex Uses only string operations with O(n) complexity - immune to ReDoS  @param value - The string to validate as a number @returns true if the string represents a valid number, false otherwise

exports.name: emailValidationFn
exports.description: Email validation function for use with react-hook-form's validate option Returns true for valid email, error message string for invalid

exports.name: numberValidationFn
exports.description: Number validation function for use with react-hook-form's validate option Returns true for valid number, error message string for invalid
